title: Outdoor Dish and Plate Rack
summary: An open-frame plate rack for a patio kitchen or outdoor bar, sized for stacked plates and hung mugs.
description:
There's very little to this build beyond a square frame and evenly spaced 1x4 shelves, which is exactly the point — it's meant to live outside near a grill or outdoor kitchen, and a simple design means fewer joints for weather to work on over time. Pocket holes do almost all the joinery, driven from the back or underside wherever possible so the visible faces stay clean.

The bottom shelf is worth a small upgrade over the plan as written: add cup hooks along its underside and it doubles as a mug rack, which is a genuinely useful addition for almost no extra material. Use exterior-rated wood and hardware throughout, and finish it before its first season outside — this rack has no roof over it in most installations, and untreated pine won't last.

tools.note: Optional for rounding or decoratively shaping the top piece.

materials.name: Cedar or pressure-treated, 1x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Extra length is built into the cut list.

materials.name: Exterior finish
materials.note: Untreated pine won't survive a season with no roof overhead.

materials.name: Cup hooks
materials.note: Optional — turns the bottom shelf into a mug rack for very little extra cost.

steps.title: Build the basic frame
steps.body: Build the basic frame from the two sides and top using 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket screws.

steps.title: Attach the bottom shelf
steps.body: Attach the bottom shelf to the sides using pocket holes. This is a good spot to add cup hooks along the underside for hanging mugs, if you'd like the added function.

steps.title: Attach the remaining shelves and rails
steps.body: Attach the remaining shelves the same way. Attach the front rails with pocket holes driven from the back side; for the top rail, add finish nails through the top face for extra hold.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand every edge and apply an exterior-rated finish before this rack sees its first season outdoors.

images.alt: Outdoor wooden plate rack with open shelves for a patio kitchen
